title: Past continuous: was/were + -ing
lesson: Use 'was/were + verb-ing' for an action in progress in the past. It often sets the scene or shows a longer action interrupted by a shorter one (past simple).

items.text: At 8 p.m. I {1} dinner.
They {1} football when it started to rain.
While I {1}, the phone rang.
items.explanation: A longer past action (was sleeping) interrupted by a shorter one (rang).

items.text: She {1} a book when I arrived.
We {1} TV when the lights went out.
He {1} fast when the police stopped him.
I {1} not {2} when you called.
What {1} you {2} at midnight?
The sun {1} when we left the house.
items.explanation: 'shine' drops the final e; sets the scene.

items.text: They {1} along the beach at sunset.
While she {1}, the cake burned.
items.explanation: A longer action interrupted by a shorter one.

items.text: I {1} a shower when the doorbell rang.
We weren't {1}; we were resting.
He {1} his bike when he fell.
The children {1} in the garden all afternoon.
While we {1}, it began to snow.
items.explanation: A longer action interrupted by a shorter one.

items.text: She {1} a letter when the lights went off.
I {1} for the bus when I saw her.
{1} they {2} when you arrived?
items.explanation: Question: were + subject + -ing; 'argue' drops the e.

items.text: He {1} when the teacher entered the room.
We {1} dinner when the guests arrived.
I wasn't {1}; I was just thinking.
She {1} her hair when the phone rang.
They {1} loudly during the film.
While he {1}, his sister borrowed his car.
items.explanation: A longer action interrupted by a shorter one.

items.text: I {1} my room when you texted me.
What {1} she {2} at that time?
We {1} not {2} attention during the lesson.
The dog {1} at the gate when I got home.
They {1} a film when the power cut out.
While I {1}, my brother cooked lunch.
items.explanation: Two past actions; the longer one uses past continuous.

items.text: She {1} a song while she did the dishes.
He {1} his keys when I called.
We {1} home when the storm began.
items.explanation: A longer action interrupted by a shorter one.
